header.location: Relocating to Portland, Oregon in May 2026

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization & Talent Development professional who thrives on designing learning programs grounded in evidence, building people-centered educational experiences, and collaborating with educators and stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate {coord|training} webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing product understanding and driving a 78% client conversion rate
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify {data|training needs} and design 3 new educational programs
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional {cross|staff}, improving {data|staff knowledge} and reducing {prog|onboarding} time by 75%
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to launch 15+ educational products, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
Led manager effectiveness strategic initiative, translating data into executive-ready reports to inform leadership decisions and strategy
Analyzed 2,000+ employee data points, communicating insights to C-suite stakeholders to guide decisions on organizational priorities
Supported leadership in advancing strategic initiatives through consultations on DEI and performance management
Coordinated 5 cross-functional projects focused on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D to improve employee experience
Initiated a {ex|DEI} pilot with an {coord|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Optimized {prog|onboarding} across 3 global locations by coordinating process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Designed and {coord|facilitated} a 3-week English immersion {prog|curriculum} for 12 students, iterating on feedback to continuously improve {data|learning outcomes}
Delivered culturally responsive lessons, adjusting content and pacing in real time to meet {ex|learner needs} and maintain engagement
Developed {data|instructional materials}—slides, worksheets, and activities—aligned to learning objectives to support knowledge retention and skill application

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology
